title: PSG-Arsenal: Paris braces for a tense weekend with Champions League final and several large concerts
subhead: 'This is an exceptional situation that calls for an exceptional operation,' said a source within Paris's police prefecture, which is planning to deploy an additional 8,000 officers to secure the Parc des Princes football stadium and an Aya Nakamura concert.

Chancellor Friedrich Merz's unpopularity fuels talk of finding a replacement
subhead: One year after taking office, Germany's head of federal government has reached record levels of unpopularity, his coalition is riven by internal disagreements and the economy is shaken. The far right's score in three regional elections in September could put him to the test.

title: US hails 'productive' talks between Israel and Lebanon
subhead: Friday's meeting between Israeli and Lebanese military officials was the first in decades. The Pentagon's second-in-command said on X that the 'productive military-to-military discussions' will 'inform the Department of State-led political track next week' and added that the US 'anticipates reconvening soon to continue the security track.'

title: 'Latte dads': The not-so-new Scandinavian fathers
subhead: Sweden is promoting a new masculine ideal: the caring, attentive father sipping matcha on a café terrace beside a stroller. But is this the sign of a real shift, or simply a reinvention of the 'new father' stereotype? So far, statistics have yet to confirm these parental feats.

title: The oil crisis suits TotalEnergies' CEO perfectly
subhead: Between price caps at the pump and controversies over windfall profits, Patrick Pouyané is once again at the center of public debate. Despite the tensions, he dreams of proving that the multinational company serves the French people's interest.
byline: Isabelle Chaperon and  Marie de Vergès

title: The resilience of French growth appears to be reaching its limits
subhead: France's gross domestic product for the first quarter of 2026 has been revised down to -0.1%. While the government blames 'wait-and-see' behavior, several indicators suggest a worsening economic climate.
